sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) delight, pleasure; Gr. ἡδονή Syr. ܥܶܕܢܐܳ only in plur. Psa 36:9 2Sa 1:24.

(2) [Eden], pr.n. of a pleasant country in Asia (the site of which is described -Gen. 2:1014 ), in which was the garden where the first created human beings were placed, Gen 2:8, 10 4:16 hence גַּן־עֵדֶן the garden of Eden, 2:15 3:23 , 24 Joe 2:3 Isa 51:3 Eze 31:9, 16 Eze 31:16. The various opinions as to the locality of the terrestrial paradise are stated and discussed by Rosenmüller, Bibl. Alterthumskunde, vol. i. p.172, seqq.; Schulthess, d. Paradies. Zurich, 1816, oct.
